brightsmith_agentAttribution.reasoning: Bronze ingest of BLS Occupational Employment & Wage Statistics (OEWS) National Cross-Industry estimates, May 2024 reference period (published March 2025). Source ZIP downloaded from the BLS special-requests endpoint, single XLSX workbook extracted, header row located by OCC_CODE+OCC_TITLE signature, OCC_GROUP/O_GROUP filter applied so only detailed-occupation rows are kept (summary major/minor/broad rollups dropped). Wage cells parsed with the BLS sentinel rules: '*' (suppressed) -> null, '#' (top-coded) -> 239200.0 with wage_capped=True. SOC codes preserved as XX-XXXX strings; non-standard 7-digit sub-classification codes (e.g. 15-1252.00) dropped. 831 rows landed in bronze.bls_oews -- within the 800-900 spec target band.

documentation.description: Ingest BLS OEWS National Cross-Industry wage percentiles (10/25/median/75/90 + mean) per detailed SOC code into bronze.bls_oews. Provides the wage distribution that complements BLS OOH's median-only point estimate, enabling career-specific salary ranges in the FutureProof UI.

dataSource.name: BLS Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics (OEWS)
dataSource.description: National cross-industry OEWS estimates, May 2024 survey vintage. ZIP archive containing a single XLSX workbook (~15MB unzipped) of one row per occupation across multiple OCC_GROUP rollup tiers. Public-domain U.S. Government Work, annual cadence (May survey, published ~March of following year).

fields.name: OCC_CODE
fields.description: SOC 2018 code in XX-XXXX format. Join key.

fields.name: O_GROUP
fields.description: Rollup tier: detailed | major | minor | broad | total. Filtered to detailed only.

dataSource.name: Local OEWS XLSX cache (fallback)
dataSource.description: Filesystem cache of the unzipped OEWS workbook. Used when the live BLS ZIP endpoint returns 403 (the BLS WAF aggressively blocks unfamiliar User-Agents). Identical content to the ZIP member.

fields.name: soc_code
fields.transformation: DIRECT from OCC_CODE; preserved as XX-XXXX string. 7-digit sub-classification codes dropped.

fields.name: total_employment
fields.transformation: DERIVED from TOT_EMP: '*' -> null; comma-stripped string -> int via round(float(.)).

fields.name: wage_annual_p10
fields.transformation: DERIVED from A_PCT10: '*' -> null; '#' -> 239200.0 (also flips wage_capped=True); numeric -> float.

fields.name: wage_annual_p25
fields.transformation: DERIVED from A_PCT25: same parser as p10.

fields.name: wage_annual_median
fields.transformation: DERIVED from A_MEDIAN: same parser as p10.

fields.name: wage_annual_p75
fields.transformation: DERIVED from A_PCT75: same parser as p10.

fields.name: wage_annual_p90
fields.transformation: DERIVED from A_PCT90: same parser as p10. Most frequently top-coded percentile.

fields.name: wage_annual_mean
fields.transformation: DERIVED from A_MEAN: same parser as p10.

fields.name: wage_hourly_median
fields.transformation: DERIVED from H_MEDIAN: same parser as p10. Reference field, not used downstream.

fields.name: wage_capped
fields.transformation: DERIVED: True iff any annual percentile (p10/p25/median/p75/p90/mean) parsed as the top-coding sentinel '#'. Hourly top-coding does not flip this flag.

dataQualityMetrics.suppressionHandling: BLS '*' suppression sentinel parsed to null on every numeric field (total_employment + all wage percentiles + wage mean + hourly median). Empty/blank cells and '**' also treated as suppressed.
dataQualityMetrics.topCodingHandling: BLS '#' sentinel on annual percentiles -> 239200.0 with wage_capped=True. Per the spec key-values table the typical top-coded row is Chief Executives (11-1011) at p90; A_PCT90 is the most-frequently-capped percentile. Hourly top-coding is a separate column-set sentinel and intentionally does NOT flip wage_capped (which is annual-only by spec).
dataQualityMetrics.summaryRowFilter: Rows with O_GROUP / OCC_GROUP != 'detailed' (case-insensitive) dropped at parse time so only ~830 detailed occupations remain. Major/minor/broad/total rollups never reach Iceberg.
dataQualityMetrics.socFormatGuard: SOC codes are regex-validated against ^\d{2}-\d{4}$. Non-conforming codes (incl. 7-digit sub-classifications like 15-1252.00) are dropped and counted via the skipped_invalid_soc warning log; 0 rows skipped on this run.
